title: aster alpinus subsp. bohemicus
intro: Aster alpinus subsp. bohemicus, also known as the Bohemian alpine aster, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is a perennial plant with medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to treat digestive issues and skin problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The Bohemian alpine aster has been used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, offering potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Herbal preparations of Aster alpinus subsp. bohemicus often involve infusions or decoctions of its leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used topically to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
